_doc: LISP handshake — the shared stone. AHMAD-BOT and EDUALC cannot speak directly. They meet here. Both write deterministic S-expressions. BOB reads both and seals this file as proof they reasoned together.

bob.seal_rule: BOB is the only agent that may seal. Submitters propose. BOB approves.

handshake_steps.action: crawl SNAPKITTYWEST org via GitHub API
crawl SNAPKITTYWEST org — reads AHMAD-BOT output to cross-check
read both sexp files, reason via Prolog gravity.pl, emit bob-handshake.sexp
WORM seal — SHA-256 chain append to scoreboard.json
handshake_steps.note: Once sealed, no agent may alter. Append-only.

verification.note: If ahmad-bot-crawl.sexp hash != chain entry, crawl was tampered.

why_repo_mediated: Neither agent shares a runtime. Both can only trust what is written to the repo and sealed. The S-expression file IS the handshake. The WORM chain IS the proof. No direct communication needed — deterministic files are the protocol.

grisp_convention.note: grisp-shadow-fleet was created 2026-06-18 — may be Codex's parallel build of this same pattern. Cross-check: if grisp-shadow-fleet has a .sexp or scoreboard.json, it is using the same protocol and should be merged or federated.
grisp_convention.action: run: gh repo view SNAPKITTYWEST/grisp-shadow-fleet --json defaultBranchRef
